chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ مِنَ الِاقْتِدَاءِ بِالْأَئِمَّةِ ( عليهم السلام ) فِي التَّعَرُّضِ لِلرِّزْقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 4 – What is Obligated from the emulating the Imams<sup>asws</sup> regarding the vulnerability of the livelihood

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/19)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/19)
text: إِنَّ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) كَانَ يَخْرُجُ وَ مَعَهُ أَحْمَالُ النَّوَى فَيُقَالُ لَهُ يَا أَبَا الْحَسَنِ مَا هَذَا مَعَكَ فَيَقُولُ نَخْلٌ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَيَغْرِسُهُ فَلَمْ يُغَادَرْ مِنْهُ وَاحِدَةٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> went out and with him<sup>asws</sup> was a load of date stones. So it was said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘O Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>! What is this with you<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Palm trees, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’. So he<sup>asws</sup> planted them, and not a single one of them failed to sprout’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ibn Mahbub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would go to work with large quantities of date stones. He was asked, ‘What are these, O abu al-Hassan?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘They are palm trees, by the will of Allah.’ He then planted them and not a single piece failed sprouting.”
